pattern: Post-order DFS processes children before parents, so a node can inspect its pruned children to decide whether it has itself become a target leaf.
hints: Process children first (post-order), replacing each child with the result of recursing into it — a child that becomes null is effectively deleted.
After updating both children, check whether the current node is now a leaf (both children null) AND has the target value; if so return null.
Returning null from the recursive call automatically severs the edge to a deleted subtree.

edge_inputs.description: single-node tree matching target — entire tree deleted
internal node becomes a leaf after its target-valued children are removed
cascading deletion up a left spine

stress.description: random complete binary tree of depth 6 with values in a small range so many deletions occur
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, size)
    nodes = [rng.randint(1, 5) for _ in range(n)]
    # Add nulls for missing children in BFS order (keep it a nearly-complete tree)
    result = []
    for v in nodes:
        result.append(v)
    target = rng.randint(1, 5)
    return [result, target]
